name: Men's Trip 2026
subtitle: The San Francisco Playbook

phases.label: Accommodations
phases.summary: Current mission: figure out where eight fraternity brothers are sleeping

phases.label: Activities
phases.summary: Rob has already put points on the board

phases.label: Restaurants & Bars
phases.summary: Plenty of important research still ahead

phases.label: Men's Dinner
phases.summary: Roland has the honor — and the pressure

accommodations.name: The Barnes San Francisco
accommodations.sleeping: Two junior suites with king bed + sofa bed, plus one room with two queen beds
accommodations.gathering: Moderate — junior suites provide sitting space
cons: Some men will use sofa beds or share beds
Less common space than a house

accommodations.name: Golden Gate Park 4BR Home
accommodations.sleeping: Four bedrooms; some men would need bunk beds

accommodations.name: The Surf Shack
accommodations.sleeping: Six or more beds; exact assignment to confirm

recommendation.title: Current clubhouse leader: The Barnes
recommendation.reason: Gus already has it locked down, it is the cheapest confirmed option, and we can bail by October 15 if a better house appears. Solid veteran move by Gus.

questions: Confirm the dates for Rob's two booked tours
Are we keeping The Barnes, or does a house win the day?
Has Roland found the legendary Men's Dinner yet?

notes.author: Bill
notes.text: Replaced generic bar images with actual venue photos and changed voting to a live leaderboard.

notes.author: Bill
notes.text: Connected bar voting to Firebase so the whole group sees the same live totals.

notes.author: Bill
notes.text: Added walkable bar options near The Barnes with simple I’m In / Pass voting.

notes.author: Bill
notes.text: Added a simple shared photo hub for viewing and uploading trip pictures.

notes.author: Bill
notes.text: Adjusted the app tone to sound more like the group: practical, brotherly, and lightly amused.

notes.author: Bill
notes.text: Clarified that Jason will wait at SFO for the group rather than going to the hotel early; he may have a drink while waiting.

notes.author: Bill
notes.text: Added incoming flight arrival times to coordinate the Wednesday meetup at SFO and the hotel.

notes.author: Rob Baker
notes.text: Booked the Alcatraz / Muir Woods / Sausalito tour and a hop-on, hop-off San Francisco bus tour. Dates are still unknown.

notes.author: Gus
notes.text: Reserved three rooms at The Barnes for $4,246 total. Cancelable through October 15.

notes.author: James
notes.text: Suggested Golden Gate Park 4BR Home and The Surf Shack as Airbnb alternatives.

responsibilities.detail: Golden Gate Park home and Surf Shack
Create the Google Photos album or Google Drive folder and add the links

arrivals.meetup: Will hold down the fort at SFO and wait for the crew — quite possibly with a cold beverage in hand.
Lands shortly before Bill and the Texas crew. Excellent timing, Roland.
The main wave. Natural group for the ride into the city.
Bringing up the rear and meeting the brotherhood at the hotel.

arrivalSummary.title: Airport game plan
arrivalSummary.text: Jason lands first and will hold down the fort at SFO, possibly with a cold beverage. Roland and Bill/Texas arrive only 22 minutes apart, setting up the main airport reunion. Avi rolls in later and will meet the brotherhood at the hotel.

photoHub.title: Trip Photos
photoHub.summary: One shared place for everyone’s pictures. See the photos, add yours, and move on with your day.
steps: Tap “See Everyone’s Pictures” to browse the shared album.
Tap “Upload My Pictures” to add photos from your phone.
You may need to sign in to Google the first time.
photoHub.note: The shared album is live. Tap either button to view or add photos.

preTrip.title: Next move
preTrip.text: Lock down the lodging, confirm Rob’s tour dates, and keep Roland focused on the Men’s Dinner.

tripComplete.title: Mission accomplished
tripComplete.text: The trip may be over, but the photo album and planning notes are still here.

events.detail: He’ll hold down the fort at the airport — likely with a cold beverage.

events.detail: American Airlines 2387 from DFW. Main airport reunion.

events.detail: He’ll meet the brotherhood at the hotel.

initial.name: Golden Gate Tap Room
initial.summary: More than 20 screens, a huge beer list, and enough games to keep eight grown men occupied.
initial.imageAlt: Pool table, televisions, seating, and bar inside Golden Gate Tap Room
initial.imageCredit: Golden Gate Tap Room photo via Thrillist

initial.name: Johnny Foley’s
initial.summary: Guinness, Irish whiskey, honest pub food, and live entertainment. A strong first-night candidate.
initial.imageAlt: Traditional wood interior, dining tables, barrels, and fireplace at Johnny Foley’s

initial.name: Bartlett Hall
initial.summary: House-brewed beer, pub food, and communal tables a few steps from the hotel.
initial.imageAlt: Communal tables, bar seating, and modern saloon interior at Bartlett Hall

bench.name: The Barnes Restaurant + Bar
bench.summary: Cocktails, shareable plates, and absolutely no argument over whose turn it is to navigate.
bench.imageAlt: Illuminated bar and seating inside The Barnes Restaurant and Bar
bench.imageCredit: The Barnes Restaurant + Bar promotional photo

bench.name: Local Edition
bench.summary: A newspaper-themed underground cocktail bar with live music and plenty of room for walk-ins.
bench.imageAlt: Stage, lounge seating, and cocktails at Local Edition
bench.imageCredit: Local Edition venue photo via Restaurant Guru

bench.name: Tempest
bench.summary: Unpretentious SoMa dive-bar energy, rotating taps, strong drinks, and comfort food.
bench.imageAlt: Neon-lit brick interior and long bar at Tempest
bench.imageCredit: Tempest photo by Kevin Kelleher and Emily Trinh / SFGATE

walkableBars.voteNote: Votes are shared live across everyone’s phones. All options stay visible and automatically sort by group preference.
